findings.issue: The section states its controlling claim early: performance gains only suggest recursion may help, while mechanism analysis investigates whether structured recursive computation appears internally.
findings.recommendation: Keep this conclusion-first framing when integrating the chapter, because it gives the reader a clear lens for all later subsections.

findings.issue: The hierarchy is coherent, moving from why performance evidence is insufficient, to PCA, fixed points and cycles, attention stability, staged inference, and takeaway. The support chain is strong, though the relationship between fixed points, cycles, and attention stability could be made slightly more explicit as a combined evidentiary bundle.
findings.recommendation: Add one linking sentence before or after the attention subsection that states the evidence stack directly: trajectory geometry plus attention stability is stronger than either observation alone.

findings.issue: The text explains PCA boundaries well, but the source-grounded evidence remains mostly timestamp-based. It refers to video observations without naming what exactly appears in the cited frames beyond the captions.
findings.recommendation: When integrating with figures, ensure captions or surrounding text describe the visible evidence precisely enough for a reader who has not seen the video.

findings.issue: The takeaway is reader-facing and accurately synthesizes the chapter into four points, then transitions to the next chapter on computational efficiency and MoR.
findings.recommendation: Preserve the transition because it helps the larger PDF maintain a forward-moving teaching arc.

audit.evaluation_context: Teaching-PDF section checkpoint: validate that this Bilibili chapter draft has a clear teaching claim, coherent subsection hierarchy, distinct same-level groups, source-grounded examples, and a reader-facing takeaway before integration.
